skill.name: pubmed-topic-recommend
skill.description: Generate ~5 actionable research topic recommendations by querying PubMed E-utilities; use when a user provides a research direction/constraints and needs evidence-backed topic ideas quickly.
skill.summary: PubMed research topic generator that queries live literature and returns evidence-backed recommendations with citations.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 99 static findings were evaluated as false positives. High-severity env_access findings (lines 30, 152-154) refer to an optional PubMed API key stored in a local config, which is standard for NCBI E-utilities rate limit improvement. The critical heuristic finding for combining code execution, network, and credential access is expected behavior for a PubMed research tool that queries the NCBI API and writes results to a local file. The 52 Ruby/shell backtick execution findings are false positives from markdown code formatting in documentation files. No malicious intent, prompt injection, or security threats were identified.

content.value_statement: Researchers spend hours searching for novel research directions without knowing which topics are timely and evidence-backed. This skill solves that by querying PubMed literature and generating actionable topic recommendations with supporting citations.
actual_capabilities: Searches PubMed using E-utilities with configurable keywords and filters
Supports date range, publication type, and exclusion filters
Produces structured JSON output with citations and rationale
Builds optimized PubMed queries with field tags and MeSH terms
limitations: Requires active Internet connection to query PubMed API
Cannot retrieve full-text article content, only metadata and abstracts
Topic quality depends on PubMed search result depth
Configuration requires editing the Python script directly

use_cases.title: New researcher needs research direction
use_cases.description: A PhD student exploring immunotherapy biomarkers receives 5 actionable topic suggestions grounded in recent PubMed literature.

use_cases.title: Grant proposal topic validation
use_cases.description: A researcher preparing a grant application validates their proposed direction against current PubMed publications.

use_cases.title: Literature gap identification
use_cases.description: A systematic review author finds underexplored sub-topics within their research area using PubMed search results.
use_cases.target_user: Systematic review teams and research analysts

prompt_templates.title: Basic topic suggestion
prompt_templates.prompt: Suggest 5 research topics on immunotherapy biomarkers for lung cancer. Use PubMed to find recent studies published in the last 3 years.
prompt_templates.scenario: A beginner user wants a quick research direction for a broad topic

prompt_templates.title: Filtered search with constraints
prompt_templates.prompt: I am researching CRISPR gene editing for cancer treatment. Exclude animal studies. Find only clinical trials and meta-analyses from the last 5 years. Generate topic recommendations with supporting citations.
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher needs focused topics with specific publication type filters

prompt_templates.title: MeSH term and cross-topic search
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a PubMed query for Alzheimer's disease biomarkers AND neuroimaging. Include MeSH terms. Limit to reviews from 2020 to 2024. Generate 5 research directions with gap statements and supporting evidence.
prompt_templates.scenario: A senior researcher needs precise MeSH-based search with cross-topic synthesis

prompt_templates.title: Complex multi-constraint search
prompt_templates.prompt: For the research area diabetes management with methodology preference AI-based prediction models, construct a PubMed search strategy. Use date filters from 2019, publication type review and clinical trial, and exclude pediatric populations. Output 5 topic recommendations each with a gap statement and at least 2 citations.
prompt_templates.scenario: An expert user needs a comprehensive search strategy with multiple constraints

output_examples.input: Find research topics on CRISPR gene editing for cancer immunotherapy.
output_examples.output: 5 topic recommendations including: CRISPR-based CAR-T cell resistance mechanisms, in vivo delivery optimization for solid tumors, CRISPR screening for novel immunotherapy targets, combination strategies with checkpoint inhibitors, and off-target effects minimization approaches. Each topic includes a gap statement and 2 supporting citations.

output_examples.input: Search for recent developments in Alzheimer's disease biomarkers published since 2020.
output_examples.output: 5 topic recommendations covering: plasma p-tau217 as a diagnostic marker, CSF proteomics for early detection, neuroimaging correlates of amyloid burden, blood-based biomarker panels for primary care screening, and digital cognitive assessment combined with fluid biomarkers. Each includes a research gap statement and supporting citations from the retrieved literature.

best_practices: Provide specific keywords and exclusion terms to improve PubMed search precision.
Set a focused date range to retrieve the most relevant and current literature.
Review the returned topics and refine search terms if results are too broad or generic.
anti_patterns: Do not use overly generic research directions without specific keywords or filters.
Do not ignore exclusion terms when searching a well-studied area.
Do not request topics beyond what the retrieved literature can support.

faq.question: What is PubMed E-utilities?
faq.answer: PubMed E-utilities is a free API provided by NCBI for programmatic access to PubMed literature.

faq.question: Do I need a PubMed API key?
faq.answer: An API key is optional. Without one, the rate is limited to 3 requests per second. With one, up to 10 requests per second.

faq.question: Can this skill access full-text articles?
faq.answer: No. It can only retrieve metadata and abstracts from PubMed.

faq.question: How many topic recommendations does it generate?
faq.answer: By default it generates 5 topic recommendations. The number is configurable in the script.

faq.question: What publication types can I filter by?
faq.answer: You can filter by review, systematic review, meta-analysis, clinical trial, and randomized controlled trial.

faq.question: Does this skill work offline?
faq.answer: No. It requires Internet access to query the PubMed API.
